verdict_reasoning: HSPA5 (BiP/GRP78) is a canonical ER-luminal HSP70 chaperone with no intrinsic membrane anchor. However, it has two documented surface-display contexts: (1) cancer-cell ecto-display — tumor cells present HSPA5 on the outer leaflet as an immunogenic target and candidate for CAR-T / ADC programs; (2) cell-death-induced externalization — apoptotic and necroptotic cells expose HSPA5 to the outer face. Both are non-baseline contexts, qualifying as cell_state_induced. The baseline NCBI compartment (ER-luminal) rules out `yes`, but documented cancer and cell-death ecto-pools rule out `no`.
predicted_key_uncertainty: The abundance and stability of HSPA5's surface pool in cancer cells vs. its canonical ER-luminal baseline is not fully quantified in routine literature; degree of clinical targeting validation (CAR-T / ADC programs) varies. The cell-death ecto-pool is well-established; cancer-cell ecto-display is documented but its extent relative to ER localization is context-dependent.
